task: Write a Python function second_largest(nums) returning the second largest distinct value in the list, or None when there are fewer than two distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly handles edge cases, including the case where there is only one distinct value.
trace: I approached this task by first converting the input list to a set, ensuring each number appears only once. Then I sorted the resulting set in reverse order using Python's built-in `sorted()` function. If there are less than two distinct values in the list (i.e., the length of the sorted set is less than 2), I return None as specified. Otherwise, the second largest number can be found at index 1 in the sorted set (since it's a reversed sorted order).
